summary: AI operating system for enterprise software modernization and S/4HANA migration.
editor_note: Legacy ERP work usually starts with consultants mapping code, dependencies, and process gaps by hand. Conduct says its AI operating system reads custom software, builds S/4HANA migration plans, and automates fit-gap analysis, refactoring, and validation. Unlike generic code assistants, it is aimed at enterprise transformation programs. The risk is whether outputs remain trusted when SAP estates are highly customized.

product_evidence.claim: The homepage positions Conduct as an AI operating system for enterprise software, including S/4HANA migration, code analysis, planning, development, and testing.
The site says Conduct reads custom code, dependencies, and downstream effects so teams can map legacy software to business functions before modernization.
The Series A note says Conduct automates template creation, fit-gap analysis, structured refactoring, and validation for complex SAP transformation programs.

market_context.primary_user: Enterprise CIO, transformation, SAP, and systems-integrator teams trying to understand and modernize customized ERP estates.
market_context.differentiation: Conduct focuses on translating legacy enterprise implementations into migration plans and executable transformation work, not only generating code snippets.
market_context.why_now: SAP customers face migration deadlines while large companies still depend on heavily customized systems that are hard to inspect manually.

risks.claim: The open question is whether enterprise teams and auditors will trust AI-generated migration maps across highly customized SAP environments.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on the official product surface and Series A announcement.
